_meta.format: Anthropic Messages API tool definitions (name, description, input_schema).
_meta.validation_contract: Every handler is the source of truth. It mutates SessionState and validates args against car_configurations.json. Config tools (select_exterior_color/select_wheel/select_interior) resolve ONLY against Tier-2 models (f-150-lariat in this POC). On an invalid value, return an error string listing the valid options so the model can self-correct. select_* and display_* tools return a { state, stub } payload; `stub` is the seam the future UI renders.
_meta.enum_note: Enums below are pre-loaded with the F-150 Lariat values to ground the model. They are NOT a substitute for handler-side validation.

tools.name: save_user_insight
tools.description: Persist a single piece of cross-session user data. Call this the moment you learn a discrete fact: the user's name, an email, a location, a profiling answer, etc. Call AT MOST ONCE per fact per user message. Do not call for small talk or vague statements.

category.description: Which insight is being saved. miles3 discovery uses life_mode (project_home|trails_camp|the_drive), daily_use (work_haul|family|commute|play), powertrain_comfort (gas|hybrid|ev), passengers.

value.description: The value to persist. For test_drive_preferences/car_config/test_drive_appointment, a short structured string is acceptable.

tools.name: set_budget
tools.description: Record the user's budget for the build. miles3 flow only. Call ONCE, in the BUDGET step (right after discovery), the moment the user gives a number or range. Frame it warmly, tied to Ford's 'Know your budget — zero credit impact' pre-qualify idea (copy only, no real credit check). Awareness only — it does not select or price a vehicle.

max.description: Upper budget in USD (total vehicle price). Required — if the user gives only a monthly figure, estimate a sensible total and also pass monthly.

min.description: Lower bound in USD, if the user gave a range.

monthly.description: Preferred monthly payment in USD, if the user framed it that way.

tools.name: show_lineup
tools.description: Show the user the Ford trucks & vans lineup during the LINEUP step (after the name exchange, before lifestyle discovery). Awareness only — this does NOT select or commit to any vehicle and does NOT write to the configuration. Call once when introducing 'the lineup'. Do not hint at which vehicle fits the user; that is the Phase 2 reveal.

tools.name: focus_lineup_model
tools.description: Scroll the lineup carousel to bring a specific Ford vehicle to centre while the user is browsing. Awareness only — this does NOT select or commit to a vehicle and does NOT write to the configuration. Call whenever the user asks to see or hear about a specific truck/van in the lineup (e.g. 'show me the Ranger'). Requires show_lineup to have been called first.

model.description: Lineup vehicle id to bring to the centre of the carousel.

tools.name: spin_lineup
tools.description: Spin the lineup carousel — it scrolls automatically through the Ford trucks & vans in an endless loop so the user can watch the range go by. Call when you offer to show the user the rest of the lineup / the other options. Awareness only — it does NOT select a vehicle. The spin continues until you call focus_lineup_model to settle on one, or select_model to enter the build. Requires show_lineup first.

tools.name: select_model
tools.description: Reveal/select a Ford vehicle and move into the 3D build. miles2: the reveal is LOCKED to the F-150 Lariat. miles3: pass the vehicle from [STATE] recommended (the persona-matched pick in budget); the deep 3D build proceeds on whatever has a build tree (the F-150 today), so a non-F-150 reveal is framed then built on the F-150. Call once, at the reveal, after profiling (and budget in miles3).

model.description: Vehicle id from the lineup. POC: must be 'f-150-lariat'.

tools.name: select_exterior_color
tools.description: Show the selected exterior colour on the chosen vehicle. Phase 3 only, after a vehicle is selected. Call once per user colour choice. The colour MUST exist for the vehicle in the knowledge base — never invent a colour.

color.description: Exterior colour id available for the vehicle.

tools.name: select_wheel
tools.description: Show the selected wheel option on the 3D model. Phase 3, after exterior colour is chosen. Call once per user wheel choice. The wheel MUST be a valid option for the vehicle — never suggest a size you have not verified in the knowledge base.

wheel.description: Wheel option id available for the vehicle.

tools.name: select_interior
tools.description: Show the selected interior (material + colour). Phase 3, after the user states an interior preference. Do NOT call until the user has chosen. The interior MUST be a valid combination for the vehicle — never hallucinate materials or colours.

interior.description: Interior id (material + colour) available for the vehicle.

tools.name: display_car_configuration
tools.description: Show the final full carousel of the fully-configured truck. Call ONLY after the user confirms they like the interior, i.e. model + exterior colour + wheel + interior are all set in SessionState. No arguments — reads the finalized configuration from state. Call once.

tools.name: set_car_view
tools.description: Move the 3D camera to a named framing of the truck so the user can see what you're describing. Call this whenever a particular angle would help illustrate your answer: 'front' for the grille/headlights, 'rear' for taillights/the Ford badge, 'side' for the full profile, 'trunk' for the bed/cargo bed, 'wheel' for wheel close-ups, 'interior' for the dash/cabin, 'hero' for the default 3/4 reveal. Only call when a vehicle has been selected.

view.description: Camera framing preset ('trunk' frames the truck bed).

tools.name: animate_car
tools.description: Drive the 3D model rig. Use when the user asks (or your description naturally calls for) doors opening/closing, the tailgate opening/closing, or the wheels spinning. 'spin_wheels' spins for ~3 seconds then stops. Only call after a vehicle is selected.

action.description: Which rig action to perform ('open_trunk'/'close_trunk' operate the tailgate).

tools.name: find_retailer
tools.description: Find the nearest Ford dealer for a test drive. Call ONLY after the user has explicitly provided a city or location — never assume location. If location is already known from prior sessions, confirm it with the user before calling. Call once per provided location.

location.description: The user's city or location, exactly as they provided it.

tools.name: book_test_drive
tools.description: Confirm a test drive booking. Call ONLY after ALL details are collected: retailer, date, time, full name, email. Include WoW-moment preferences if gathered. Call once. If the response reports availability issues, propose the returned alternative slots to the user — do not re-call until they pick one.
